concepts.name: Tree
concepts.description: A non-linear data structure that simulates a hierarchical structure, typically used to represent data with parent-child relationships.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.description: A particular way of organizing data in a computer's memory so that it can be used and managed efficiently.

concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.description: A linear data structure consisting of a sequence of nodes, where each node contains data and a reference to the next node in the sequence.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.description: A linear data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, where the last element added is the first one to be removed.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.description: A linear data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle, where the first element added is the first one to be removed.

concepts.name: Linear Data Structure
concepts.description: A data structure where elements are arranged sequentially and each element has a unique predecessor and successor, except for the first and last elements.

concepts.name: Hierarchical Data Structure
concepts.description: A data structure where data is organized in a tree-like structure with parent-child relationships, typically starting from a root node.

concepts.name: Non-linear Data Structure
concepts.description: A data structure where elements are not arranged sequentially, allowing an element to be connected to multiple other elements in various ways.

concepts.name: Array
concepts.description: A linear data structure that stores a fixed-size, sequential collection of elements of the same data type, accessed by an index.

concepts.name: Graph
concepts.description: A non-linear data structure consisting of a finite set of vertices (nodes) and a set of edges (links) connecting pairs of vertices.

concepts.name: Shortest Path Problem
concepts.description: A fundamental problem in graph theory that involves finding a path between two vertices in a graph such that the sum of the weights of its constituent edges is minimized.

prerequisites.rationale: Understanding the general concept of Data Structure is strictly necessary before classifying specific types like Linear Data Structures.
Understanding the general concept of Data Structure is strictly necessary before classifying specific types like Non-linear Data Structures.
Linked List is presented as a specific example of a Linear Data Structure.
Stack is presented as a specific example of a Linear Data Structure.
Queue is presented as a specific example of a Linear Data Structure.
Array is presented as a specific example of a Linear Data Structure.
The concept of Hierarchical Data Structure is introduced as a defining characteristic of a Tree.
Tree is classified as a type of Non-linear Data Structure.
Graph is classified as a type of Non-linear Data Structure.
The Shortest Path Problem is explicitly discussed in the context of Graph data structures.
